nodes.description_short: Chechen village burned by Russians; Sado's home, site of atrocity.
nodes.dramatic_use: Moral core of the sequence; senseless destruction triggers vengeance.

nodes.description_short: Advanced Russian military fort ten miles from Makhket; barracks, Shahgirinsk Gate, Vorontsov's quarters within.
nodes.dramatic_use: Russian command center; hinge between Chechen wilderness and imperial society

aliases: where the Russians would be felling trees beyond the Mitchik
nodes.description_short: Forest glade beyond the Mitchik river where Russians fell trees; mist, bonfires, sharpshooter line.
nodes.dramatic_use: Site of Hadji Murad's formal surrender to Vorontsov and Avdeev's mortal wounding

nodes.description_short: Vorontsov's palace in Tiflis: porch, drawing room, cabinet, waiting room, ballroom.
nodes.dramatic_use: Theatre of imperial power, flattery, and the first Vorontsov-Hadji Murad interview.

nodes.description_short: Caucasian capital and seat of Viceroy Vorontsov; political and social center of the campaign.
nodes.dramatic_use: Imperial counterweight to the mountains; polished court masking military failure.

nodes.description_short: Cliffside path with hundred-and-twenty-yard abyss where Hadji Murad leaped.
nodes.dramatic_use: Iconic escape that scars his leg and makes his legend

nodes.description_short: Lofty four-windowed audience room with Alexander I portrait and writing table.
nodes.dramatic_use: Chamber of capricious imperial decrees on Hadji Murad and the Pole

nodes.description_short: Shamil's capital aoul where HM's family is held after defection.
nodes.dramatic_use: Counter-court to the Russian fort; Shamil's power seat.

nodes.description_short: Room where six elders convene; Yusuf is summoned and threatened.
nodes.dramatic_use: Political theatre; Yusuf-Shamil confrontation decides HM's fate.

nodes.description_short: Small Mohammedan town in Transcaucasia; Hadji Murat's assigned residence.
nodes.dramatic_use: Hadji Murat's gilded cage; site of decision to flee; escape starts here.

nodes.description_short: Five-room house near mosque and Khan's palace housing Hadji Murat.
nodes.dramatic_use: Murat prays, receives messengers, decides to escape; slaps Kirillov.

nodes.description_short: Shrub-covered rise where Murat makes last stand in old ditch.
nodes.dramatic_use: Final battlefield; Hamzad parallel fulfilled; death of Hadji Murat.

nodes.description_short: Ditch among shrubs dug into a barricade with daggers.
nodes.dramatic_use: Murat's last defensive position; two wounds taken here.

aliases: second saklya cut into the hillside
nodes.description_short: Clay-plastered whitewashed Chechen house with penthouse, cushions, weapons on nails; hidden hospitality.
nodes.dramatic_use: Site of Hadji Murad's secret planning with Sado, dispatch of envoy to Russians, kunak bond

nodes.description_short: Village mosque where Hadji Murad and Osman assassinated Hamzad during feast.
nodes.dramatic_use: Blood-revenge climax against Hamzad's murids

nodes.description_short: Carpeted, richly curtained drawing room lit by four candles; card table, champagne, Petersburg-level luxury in a Caucasian fort.
nodes.dramatic_use: Reveals Vorontsov domestic world; Marya's flirtation with Poltoratsky; envoy's arrival announced

nodes.description_short: Abstract mountainous region; home of Avars, Shamil, Murat's roots.
nodes.dramatic_use: Goal of escape; symbolic homeland; forever unreachable.

nodes.description_short: Frozen Russian village threshing floor where Avdeev's family flails oats at dawn.
nodes.dramatic_use: Rural Russia's rhythm of labor; the home Avdeev is dying for.

nodes.description_short: Ballroom with hidden band, champagne buffet, half-dressed women whirling.
nodes.dramatic_use: Western decadence confronting Islamic austerity; Hadji Murad's silent indifference.

nodes.description_short: Interior suite with drawing room and bedroom where Hadji Murad dictates his life story.
nodes.dramatic_use: Intimate chamber for Loris-Melikov interviews and murid tensions

nodes.description_short: Room across hall where Murat's four murids sleep, sharpen weapons.
nodes.dramatic_use: Preparation for escape; Khanefi sings Hamzad's song (foreshadowing).

nodes.description_short: Seat of the Avar Khans where Hadji Murad grew up in palace intimacy.
nodes.dramatic_use: Stage of the Khans' massacre and Hamzad's assassination

nodes.description_short: Murid encampment on a hill where Hamzad received and murdered the Khans.
nodes.dramatic_use: Site of treacherous ambush that reshapes Hadji Murad's fate

nodes.description_short: Imperial capital where Chernyshov delivers the Vorontsov report to Nicholas.
nodes.dramatic_use: Seat of autocratic power deciding Hadji Murad's fate

nodes.description_short: Petersburg residence of Nicholas I with rebuilt rooms and ministerial cabinet.
nodes.dramatic_use: Architectural symbol of Nicholas's vanity and power

nodes.description_short: Russian frontier fort where Petrov commands and Hadji Murat stays.
nodes.dramatic_use: Primary setting of HM's Russian internment; domestic war-theatre.

nodes.description_short: Major Ivan Matveich Petrov's quarters, lived in with Marya Dmitrievna.
nodes.dramatic_use: Hosts Hadji Murat; crucible of cross-cultural intimacy.

nodes.description_short: Dark fetid pit over seven feet deep holding Yusuf and seven criminals.
nodes.dramatic_use: Visceral image of Yusuf's suffering; lever Shamil will pull.

nodes.description_short: Complex of inner/outer courts and harem at Vedeno.
nodes.dramatic_use: Seat of imamate authority; mirror to Russian fort.

nodes.description_short: Small Russian fort where Butler lodges with Major Petrov and Marya Dmitrievna.
nodes.dramatic_use: Butler's moral decline; site of Hadji Murat's severed head being displayed.

nodes.description_short: Private room where Hadji Murat prays and deliberates through the night.
nodes.dramatic_use: Internal monologue; falcon fable; fateful decision.

nodes.description_short: Bright moonlit road between fort houses where Butler meets Marya Dmitrievna.
nodes.dramatic_use: Intimate encounter; Kamenev arrives with the head.

nodes.description_short: Spring-flooded bog where horses mire and escape fails.
nodes.dramatic_use: Physical trap; poetic death-bog; plan collapses.

nodes.description_short: Narrator's ploughed field with crushed thistle framing the novella.
nodes.dramatic_use: Closes the frame: Murat's death = the mown thistle.

aliases: where the river Argun bends by the cliff
nodes.description_short: Forest clearing by the Argun river bend with two haystacks and bonfire; hiding place for Hadji Murad's murids.
nodes.dramatic_use: Hadji Murad's rendezvous camp before crossing to Russian lines

nodes.description_short: Blackened broken plane tree in night forest, customary outpost site for Russian soldiers.
nodes.dramatic_use: Setting for Panov/Avdeev/Nikitin/Bondarenko ambush scene; first Russian foot-soldier POV

aliases: bare descending slope of a ravine
nodes.description_short: Open ravine between Russian outpost line and young forest where Chechen horsemen appear.
nodes.dramatic_use: Tactical space of the skirmish in which Avdeev is shot

nodes.description_short: Hostile mountainous region targeted by Russian raid January 1852.
nodes.dramatic_use: Theatre of imperial violence; home of the burned aoul and Shamil's power base.

nodes.description_short: Small wooden hospital at the fort's entrance where the wounded Avdeev dies.
nodes.dramatic_use: Quiet deathbed counterpoint to diplomatic drama; the human cost of empire.

nodes.description_short: Palace dining room where thirty guests gossip about Hadji Murad.
nodes.dramatic_use: Social stage where the Dargo disaster is re-mythologized as triumph.

nodes.description_short: Public masked ball with reserved velvet box for the Emperor's trysts.
nodes.dramatic_use: Scene of Nicholas's predatory encounter with the Swedish girl

nodes.description_short: Petersburg quayside walk in 13-below frost where Nicholas meets the student.
nodes.dramatic_use: Public stage of Nicholas's petty cruelty and narcissism

nodes.description_short: Reception room where Petrov meets HM and farewell tea is served.
nodes.dramatic_use: Formal meeting and farewell; burka-gift scene.

nodes.description_short: Steps of the major's house; HM arrives and later is ambushed here.
nodes.dramatic_use: Liminal threshold; site of Arslan Khan assassination attempt.

aliases: the saklya where the women are kept
nodes.description_short: House where HM's mother, two wives and five children are guarded.
nodes.dramatic_use: Captivity of HM's family; stakes of his defection.

nodes.description_short: Military encampment where troops fell forest; Vorontsov's tent and dinner.
nodes.dramatic_use: Farewell dinner for Kozlovsky; Butler gambles away his savings; aristocratic idyll in war zone.

nodes.description_short: Officer's tent housing card table where Butler is ruined.
nodes.dramatic_use: Butler loses 470 rubles at cards; breaks his vow.

nodes.description_short: Road leaving fort past women, soldiers, buffalo carts toward mountains.
nodes.dramatic_use: Escape begins; chase accelerates.

nodes.description_short: Ravine below the aul with running water; Hadji Murad crosses under pursuit.
nodes.dramatic_use: Action-chase beat of Hadji Murad's escape from his pursuers

nodes.description_short: Mountainous homeland of the Avars contested between Khans, Shamil and Russians.
nodes.dramatic_use: Political stake behind Hadji Murad's shifting allegiances

nodes.description_short: Private chamber in Vorontsov's house where Hadji Murad prays and waits on guard.
nodes.dramatic_use: Reveals his hidden fear and distrust beneath diplomatic composure.

nodes.description_short: Peasant cottage with stove and frozen windowpanes, the Avdeev family home.
nodes.dramatic_use: Domestic Russia in miniature; where the letter is dictated, where grief will land.

nodes.description_short: Avdeev's Russian peasant village, implied setting of threshing, hut, church.
nodes.dramatic_use: The forgotten home front; prayers and lamentations for the war dead.

nodes.description_short: Oriental-style Tiflis theater hosting an Italian opera attended by Hadji Murad.
nodes.dramatic_use: Exoticized spectacle; Hadji Murad's impassive gaze marks cultural gulf.

nodes.description_short: Small mountain aoul, 'size of an ass's head', Hadji Murad's birthplace.
nodes.dramatic_use: Origin of Hadji Murad and site of Akhmet Khan's later siege

nodes.description_short: Royal residence of the Avar Khans inside Khunzakh, site of courtly childhood.
nodes.dramatic_use: Setting for dynastic intrigue and Hamzad's takeover

nodes.description_short: Simple mountain dwelling where Hadji Murad's mother nursed and hid her scar.
nodes.dramatic_use: Sensory flashback of maternal sacrifice and lullaby

nodes.description_short: Imperial frontier theater of perpetual war debated in Tiflis and Petersburg.
nodes.dramatic_use: Strategic abstraction weighed by Vorontsov, Chernyshov and Nicholas

nodes.description_short: Formal dining room where Nicholas discusses Hadji Murad with ambassadors.
nodes.dramatic_use: Site of the 'two cauteries' political bon mot

nodes.description_short: Spartan chamber with hard bed and Napoleon-comparison cloak.
nodes.dramatic_use: Private space of self-mythologizing insomnia

nodes.description_short: Long sled relay where couriers beat drivers bloody to deliver reports.
nodes.dramatic_use: Physical toll connecting Caucasus and throne

nodes.description_short: Road through tilled fields and meadows toward forest-clad hills.
nodes.dramatic_use: Opening war tableau; establishes Butler's romantic view of war.

nodes.description_short: Dark forest from which Chechen horsemen emerge with banners.
nodes.dramatic_use: Site of ambush; reveals Shamil's green banner.

nodes.description_short: Valley where Chechen horsemen descend and are repulsed by artillery.
nodes.dramatic_use: Combat engagement site; first contact.

nodes.description_short: Courtyard of petitioners guarded by two armed Lesghians.
nodes.dramatic_use: Stages Shamil's stony authority as he ignores the crowd.

nodes.description_short: Inner court with penthouse where Shamil dismounts; harem behind fence.
nodes.dramatic_use: Private/public divide; Aminal peeks from women's side.

nodes.description_short: Row of tents with long table set like a Petersburg Guards dinner.
nodes.dramatic_use: Kozlovsky's tearful farewell speech; performative Russian aristocracy.

nodes.description_short: Garden outside Murat's house filled with singing nightingales.
nodes.dramatic_use: Nightingale motif bookending death; beauty against violence.

nodes.description_short: Midsummer Russian field with wildflowers and a resilient crimson Tartar thistle in a ditch.
nodes.dramatic_use: Frame narrator's prologue; thistle as metaphor for Hadji Murad's tenacity

nodes.description_short: Misty forest road six miles beyond Shahgirinsk Gate, blocked with felled trees and bonfires.
nodes.dramatic_use: Staging ground for Poltoratsky's company; approach corridor to surrender site

nodes.description_short: Russian fortress near Chechnya chosen as base for Hadji Murad's family negotiations.
nodes.dramatic_use: Operational launchpad for ransom scheme

nodes.description_short: Site of the disastrous 1845 Vorontsov campaign disguised as a 'rescue'.
nodes.dramatic_use: Hidden shame haunting Vorontsov's court; Hadji Murad's dangerous memory.

nodes.description_short: Mountain region Hadji Murad vows to raise against Shamil.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographic stake of the war; Hadji Murad's promised battlefield.

nodes.description_short: Russian military garrison town; destination of Hadji Murad's chained transport.
nodes.dramatic_use: Intended Russian prison aborted by his leap

nodes.description_short: Hall where hundred courtiers await Nicholas's New Year greetings.
nodes.dramatic_use: Spectacle of autocratic self-display

nodes.description_short: Petersburg ballet where Nicholas orders a diamond ring for a dancer.
nodes.dramatic_use: Closing beat of imperial appetite and whim

aliases: place where Nicholas usually had rendezvous
nodes.description_short: Designated secret lodging where Nicholas takes women for assignations.
nodes.dramatic_use: Hidden venue of the Emperor's hypocrisy

nodes.description_short: Ever-changing snowy peaks on the lofty horizon like diamonds.
nodes.dramatic_use: Lyrical counterpoint to violence; Butler's poetic horizon.

nodes.description_short: Grandfather's burnt beehives, apricot and cherry trees, scorched.
nodes.dramatic_use: Emblem of patient labor destroyed; stoic grandfather motif.

nodes.description_short: Marya Dmitrievna rolls pastry with sleeves up when HM arrives.
nodes.dramatic_use: First domestic glimpse; Marya's unglamorous power.

nodes.description_short: Female domain behind the fence where Shamil seeks Aminal.
nodes.dramatic_use: Humanizes Shamil; domestic humiliation by Aminal's refusal.

nodes.description_short: Kumukh aoul friendly to Russians that invited HM to its mosque.
nodes.dramatic_use: Trigger for Arslan Khan's blood-feud ambush.

nodes.description_short: Viceroy's Tiflis residence where Hadji Murat seeks audience.
nodes.dramatic_use: Repeated futile audiences; Hadji Murat's trap tightens.

nodes.description_short: River Murat intends to cross to mislead pursuers.
nodes.dramatic_use: Unreachable barrier; strategic feint that fails.

nodes.description_short: Gate of Vozdvizhensk Fort through which ambush parties and wood-felling companies depart.
nodes.dramatic_use: Threshold from Russian safety into contested forest

nodes.description_short: Small shared officer's cabin inside the fort; Vovilo drunk-asleep at the door, Tikhonov in bed.
nodes.dramatic_use: Comic bathetic counterpoint to the drawing-room elegance; shows Poltoratsky's infatuation

nodes.description_short: Village church where a requiem is chanted for Peter Avdeev.
nodes.dramatic_use: Ritual closure; Orthodox faith folding the soldier's death into routine.

nodes.description_short: Drawing-room corner where Vorontsov plays ombre after dinner.
nodes.dramatic_use: Intimate courtier ritual, snuff-box and Alexander I portrait, arrival of son's letter.

aliases: the quarters that had been assigned to him
nodes.description_short: Lodging assigned to Hadji Murad in Tiflis, reached by phaeton after the ball.
nodes.dramatic_use: His gilded cage; escape from the ball into Mohammedan solitude.

nodes.description_short: Frontier line in Daghestan from which Hadji Murad wants to raise a revolt.
nodes.dramatic_use: Strategic promise; the plan that will never be granted.

nodes.description_short: Village fountain deliberately polluted so water cannot be used.
nodes.dramatic_use: Detail of calculated cruelty; water as life taken away.

nodes.description_short: Region Shamil dispatched Hadji Murad to, where he seized sheep and horses.
nodes.dramatic_use: Pretext for Shamil's suspicion and final rupture

nodes.description_short: Khanate ruled in turn by Mahomet-Murza then Akhmet Khan under Russian appointment.
nodes.dramatic_use: Source of Akhmet Khan's hatred driving the betrayal

nodes.description_short: Antechamber where Chernyshov and Dolgoruky are met by the aide-de-camp.
nodes.dramatic_use: Threshold ritual emphasizing courtly servility

nodes.description_short: Court chapel where the deacon chants 'Many Years' for Nicholas.
nodes.dramatic_use: Religious mirror of imperial self-worship

nodes.description_short: Dusty street of the fort where HM arrives and departs.
nodes.dramatic_use: Public sightline for HM arrivals/departures.

nodes.description_short: Porch-like overhang where HM's wives watch Shamil ride in.
nodes.dramatic_use: Captive spectators framed under Shamil's gaze.

nodes.description_short: Young Vorontsov's luxurious tent where Marya Vasilevna stays.
nodes.dramatic_use: Emblem of aristocratic excess amid combat.

nodes.description_short: Aoul with saklyas and minarets visible to the right of the road.
nodes.dramatic_use: Decision point; Murat chooses left toward Alazan.

nodes.description_short: Russian imperial capital, invoked as origin of Marya Vasilevna's beauty and the tutor's education.
nodes.dramatic_use: Distant metropolis that measures the couple's sense of 'privation' at the fort

nodes.description_short: Winter sledge road from the village to town, three sledges of oats.
nodes.dramatic_use: Quiet transit of commerce and the doomed letter.

aliases: palace on the south coast of the Crimea
nodes.description_short: Vorontsov's lavish Crimean estate built with his wife's wealth.
nodes.dramatic_use: Emblem of the Viceroy's opulence and European tastes.

nodes.description_short: Remote farm where the silver-hammering grandfather lived and received the child.
nodes.dramatic_use: Memory of ancestral craft and kinship

nodes.description_short: Fort near hostile settlement ruled out because Vorontsov's son no longer commands it.
nodes.dramatic_use: Rejected alternative underscoring kunak bond with young Vorontsov

nodes.description_short: Western border where Nicholas orders two divisions staged against unrest.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographic proof of Nicholas's reactionary politics

nodes.description_short: Clear rapid stream forded on approach and return from the raid.
nodes.dramatic_use: Natural threshold between safety and hostile forest.

nodes.description_short: Aoul where Yusuf lived the easy life of a naib's son.
nodes.dramatic_use: Backstory location grounding Yusuf's admiration for Shamil.

nodes.description_short: Mosque HM was invited to but forbidden by princes and Russians.
nodes.dramatic_use: Off-screen flashpoint fuelling the assassination attempt.

nodes.description_short: Mohammedan mosque near Murat's house for ritual prayer.
nodes.dramatic_use: Reason Murat requests Nukha; religious anchor.

nodes.description_short: Watchtower from which alarm shot is fired.
nodes.dramatic_use: Triggers pursuit; bureaucratic war machine wakes.

nodes.description_short: River in Hamzad's song crossed by pursuing Russian prince.
nodes.dramatic_use: Mythic parallel to Murat's coming death.

nodes.description_short: Shamil's stronghold where a captured Russian soldier was sent — offscreen reference.
nodes.dramatic_use: Anchors Shamil's power base geographically

nodes.description_short: Named river whose cliff-bend marks the forest rendezvous.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographic anchor for the secret meeting point

nodes.description_short: River beyond which the wood-cutting expedition and Shalin glade lie.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographic marker delimiting Russian operational territory

nodes.description_short: Provincial town inn where the old man has the letter read aloud.
nodes.dramatic_use: Brief waystation showing peasant reliance on literate intermediaries.

nodes.description_short: 1812 battlefield where Vorontsov was credited with conquering Napoleon.
nodes.dramatic_use: Backstory prestige; source of the White Cross and imperial legitimacy.

nodes.description_short: Mountain village captured by mountaineers in 1843, site of Hadji Murad's past exploit.
nodes.dramatic_use: Flashback reference establishing Hadji Murad's battlefield legend.

aliases: village of Akhmet Khan of Mekhtuli
nodes.description_short: Khanate whose widowed khansha Hadji Murad seized in a night raid.
nodes.dramatic_use: Anecdote positioning Hadji Murad as audacious avenger.

nodes.description_short: Daghestani town Hadji Murad plundered in 1849 in broad daylight.
nodes.dramatic_use: Further legend-building episode recounted by Armenian guest.

nodes.description_short: Gallery Nicholas traverses en route to Minister Volkonski's apartments.
nodes.dramatic_use: Transit beat weaving through palace routine

nodes.description_short: Side entrance of the Winter Palace where Helena Pavlovna's carriage arrives.
nodes.dramatic_use: Trigger for Nicholas's contempt of liberal relatives

nodes.description_short: Where Butler sleeps dreamlessly after the raid-day dinner.
nodes.dramatic_use: Closes Ch.16 with innocence/oblivion.

nodes.description_short: Soldiers' quarters where Kabarda guests are fed and given vodka.
nodes.dramatic_use: Military hospitality ritual; contrast to officers' debauchery.

nodes.description_short: Imperial capital invoked by dinner ambience and Vorontsov's letters.
nodes.dramatic_use: Off-screen center of power; distant authority.

nodes.description_short: Lower village spring where women and children gather at dusk.
nodes.dramatic_use: Atmospheric sound-scape of the aul at Hadji Murad's arrival

nodes.description_short: Neighboring Chechen aul whose hay was burned by Russians — mentioned, not shown.
nodes.dramatic_use: Offscreen marker of Russian reprisals fueling mountaineer grievance

nodes.description_short: Second village mentioned as destination for an envoy — brief reference only.
nodes.dramatic_use: Node in Hadji Murad's clandestine communication network

nodes.description_short: Russian southern coastal region, site of Vorontsov's palace and garden.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographic mark of imperial luxury, far from the Caucasus war.

nodes.description_short: Administrative district commanded by Prince Tarkhanov, consulted on Hadji Murad.
nodes.dramatic_use: Bureaucratic reference situating Tarkhanov

nodes.description_short: Distant homeland whose respect Butler imagines earning.
nodes.dramatic_use: Invisible audience Butler performs for.

nodes.description_short: Palace landmark near Murat's assigned house.
nodes.dramatic_use: Spatial landmark; local power.

nodes.description_short: Region containing Nukha and Tiflis.
nodes.dramatic_use: Geographical parent for Murat's exile.

nodes.description_short: Fort from which visiting officers arrive at Butler's fort.
nodes.dramatic_use: Off-screen origin of dinner guests; spatial network.
